entries.definition:
ه/ر/ب h–r–b to escape, to flee, to desert; to seek refuge; refuge;
fugitive; to disappear from sight; to become feeble. Of this root,
هَرَب harab occurs once in the Qur’an.

هَرَب harab [v. n.] fleeing, running away (72:12) وَأَنَّا ظَنَنَّا أَنْ لَنْ
نُعْجِزَ اللَّهَ فِي الْأَرْضِ وَلَنْ نُعْجِزَهُ هَرَبًا we know we can never frustrate
God on earth; we can never frustrate Him [by] fleeing.

هاروت Hārūt (see alphabetically).
